brief_summary: Top-down keyboard outline diagram marking three coral zones that tolerate a profile swap — a single accent key, the modifier row either side of the spacebar, and the spacebar itself — against a bordeaux-dashed alpha block and number row labeled as the zone to leave in the base profile.
data_sources: mixing-keycap-profiles.mdx § 'Where the swap actually lands' — 'Builders who mix profiles successfully are not reshuffling the whole layout. They are working three zones: a single accent key (usually Esc or the arrow cluster), the modifier row, and the spacebar. Everywhere else — the alpha block, the number row — stays in the base profile'
